% Lipschitz-hypothesis probe on MNIST-CNN: ratio Δg·σ/‖δ‖ vs ‖δ‖. ratio≤1 ⇔ (1/σ)-Lipschitz.

(0.750,0.141) (1.000,0.106) (0.100,0.000) (0.200,0.000) (0.300,0.000) (0.500,0.000) (0.750,0.133) (1.000,0.195)};
\addlegendentry{$\sigma=0.5$}
\end{axis}
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

\noindent Empirical grounding of the randomized-smoothing certificate on MNIST-CNN: each
point shifts a test input by a known $\|\delta\|_2$ and measures the resulting change in
the probit score $g_{\hat c}=\Phi^{-1}(P[f(x+\eta)=\hat c])$, normalized by the bound
$\|\delta\|/\sigma$. The $(1/\sigma)$-Lipschitz hypothesis of
\texttt{smoothing\_certified\_radius} is exactly ``every point $\le 1$'' (dashed):
$717/720$ measurements satisfy it; the few above (smallest $\|\delta\|$, largest
$\sigma$) are within Monte-Carlo error of the finite-sample $\hat p$. The assumption the
proof takes for granted is here \emph{measured} on the trained net.
